# Copilot Instructions for KnowledgeBase



## Оглавление


## Project Overview


## Structure and Organization
- The repository is organized into directories based on content type (e.g., `interview-questions`, `best-practices`, `resources`).
- Each directory contains Markdown files that cover specific topics or questions.
- Files are named descriptively to indicate their content (e.g., `typescript-best-practices.md`, `react-interview-questions.md`).


## Content Formatting Conventions
- Use Markdown syntax for formatting.
- Use headings (H1, H2, H3) to organize content hierarchically.
- Use bullet points for lists and key takeaways.
- Include links to relevant resources and documentation.
- Use consistent naming conventions for files and sections.
- For technical content, include code snippets and examples where applicable.
- For interview questions, provide clear and concise answers with explanations.

## Contribution Guidelines
- Ensure all contributions align with the project's goals and standards.
- Review existing documentation to avoid duplication.
- Provide clear and descriptive commit messages when making changes.
- Collaborate with other contributors for feedback and improvements.
- Regularly update the documentation to reflect changes in technology and best practices.

## Workflows
- Edit files directly in VS Code; no build process required.
- Use Git for version control: commit changes with descriptive messages.
- No automated tests; manually verify links and formatting.

## Key References
- [GitHub Markdown Guide](https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/)
- [VS Code Documentation](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs)
- [GitHub Documentation](https://docs.github.com/)
